chunks.title: Introduction to Sun Exposure
chunks.summary: The speaker introduces the topic of sun exposure and its effects on health without delving deeply into skin cancer or sunscreen issues. They emphasize the importance of recognizing the role of sun exposure in enhancing certain biological functions.
chunks.transcript: okay we've covered a lot one of the last things i want to talk about is sun exposure and this is not a full podcast about sun exposure skin cancer risk skin damage ultraviolet light i've done previous podcasts on that i can go back down the rabbit hole sunscreen yes is mostly it's full of garbage octocrylene ava benzone homogeneous all kinds of garbage things in sunscreen that are excreted in your poop and your pee and cause cancer sunscreen is  if you're gonna use a sunscreen use something that's animal based with animal fats and zinc we're developing something stay tuned for that it's going to be a bit because sunscreens are hard to build but we are building a good sunscreen for you guys in the

chunks.title: Sunscreen Ingredients and Recommendations
chunks.summary: A critical view is presented regarding common sunscreen ingredients, which are described as harmful. The speaker suggests using animal-based sunscreens with natural components like animal fats and zinc.

chunks.title: Benefits of Sun Exposure on Hormones
chunks.summary: The speaker outlines research indicating that sun exposure may positively influence hormones, including testosterone levels. They highlight studies linking sunlight to enhanced sexual behavior and romantic passion.
chunks.transcript: there is some evidence though the research is not completely clear on this there's some good evidence that sun exposure is associated with positive increases in the gonadal axis so skin exposure to uvb light increase induces a skin brain gonad access and sexual behavior this is from cell reports i'm not sure you get any more reputable than a cell journal the graphical abstract here says that perhaps this is involved with p53 gene activity and they are looking at animal models but they are saying that there is good evidence that solar exposure in humans enhances romantic passion and positively correlates with male testosterone levels so i would say if you are not getting in the sun do that if you want more testosterone or if you want improved hormones as a man or a woman there's probably a good reason that no one takes honeymoon and i

chunks.title: Personal Experience with Sunlight
chunks.summary: Sharing personal anecdotes, the speaker discusses the benefits they experience from sun exposure, including increased libido and a natural tan. They address common concerns about skin cancer and emphasize their confidence in sun health.
chunks.transcript: northern places people go on honeymoons to warm places with lots of sun and who knows maybe you get in the sun and you feel a little more uh libidinous a little more horny and that's a great thing in your honeymoon or any time that you want to have sex with your romantic partner because this is the place to do it i'm this the place to do it i mean i'm sitting at the ninth latitude in costa rica and i can tell you it's great here there's a lot of sun and i think people are quite um healthy here from at least a sun perspective uh and i do put myself in the sun quite a bit if you've any seen any of my stories or any of my social media you know that i'm quite tan people will sometimes say aren't you worried about skin cancer to which i say no not at all because i don't have much linoleic and acid in my cell membranes at all but what i do benefit from is a healthy libido because of that sun

chunks.title: Seasonal Effects on Hormonal Levels
chunks.summary: The speaker references a study suggesting seasonal variations in hormone levels in men, particularly emphasizing the differences observed between summer and winter. This is linked back to the overall benefits of being in sunny environments.
chunks.transcript: that i will mention here is the uh this study from the journal of clinical endocrinology and metabolism and it shows the variation in levels of serum inhibin a which is a feedback loop remember from the sertoli cells testosterone estradiol luteinizing hormone follicle stimulating hormone and sex hormone binding globulin in monthly samples from healthy men during a 17 month period possible effects of the season i will just tell you in brief that the seasonal variation does appear to be real at least in this study and this is a norwegian population who may see more variation in the days between the winter and the summer but i think that most of us will at least subjectively agree that being in warm climates being tan getting in the sun does improve libido as long as you don't overdo it and get tanned and then get really tired because
